prompt:
We publish the employee handbook as static HTML, and accessibility fixes only happen when someone complains, because nobody can point at a line number in review. I want a CI audit step that reads a rendered page and emits findings a reviewer can act on: a11y_audit.py, stdlib only, built on html.parser (its getpos() gives you the 1-based line of every start tag — that is exactly the line number I want on each finding; for a tag spread over several source lines, the line where it opens). One export: audit(html) -> list of findings. Each finding compares equal to a plain 3-tuple (code, line, subject) — a namedtuple is the friendly choice. The list is sorted by (line, code, subject), so a stable diff-able report falls out for free. The five checks, with their exact codes: 1. "heading-skip" — walk h1..h6 in document order and compare each heading's level to the PREVIOUS heading's level, with the start of the document counting as level 0 (so a page whose first heading is an h2 gets flagged). A heading more than one level deeper than its predecessor is a skip: flag it at its own line with the tag as subject (e.g. "h3"). Going back UP any number of levels is fine, and the comparison is always against the immediately previous heading, wherever it was. 2. "img-missing-alt" — an img with no alt attribute at all. alt="" is a deliberate decorative marker and passes. Subject: the src value, or "" when there is no src. Self-closing counts like any other (html.parser reports those through a different handler — don't lose them). 3. "label-orphan" — a label with a non-empty for="x" where no input, select, or textarea anywhere in the document has id="x". Anywhere means anywhere: the control may appear after the label, so resolve after the whole document is read. An id on a div or span does NOT satisfy a label — labels must point at a form control. Labels without a for attribute are fine (they wrap their control). Subject: the for value. 4. "duplicate-id" — an id value used by more than one element (any elements). Flag the SECOND and later occurrences, each at its own line, subject: the id value. First occurrence stays clean. Empty id="" never counts, and comparison is case-sensitive ("Intro" and "intro" are different ids). 5. "landmark-missing" — the page has no
element at all: exactly one finding, pinned at line 1 with subject "main". An empty document gets this finding too. No other checks, no severity levels, no config — five codes, deterministic output. python3 test_a11y_audit.py is the acceptance gate and the test file is read-only. Tag/attribute case: html.parser lowercases names for you; id VALUES keep their case.
